title: Chris Fields — Quantum Foundations and Measurement (Collected)
subtitle: 6 papers on the measurement problem, quantum biology, classical-quantum boundary, and the role of the observer

summary.one_line: Fields' work on the measurement problem shows that measurement is not a physical interaction but the establishment of a classical record — the observer does not cause collapse; the observer IS the ...
summary.scope: Covers quantum measurement, decoherence, quantum biology, classical-quantum boundary.

sources.label: Chris Fields — Collected Papers. 6 PDFs in /root/projects/blog/library/science/authors/fields/.

body.text: Fields argues that quantum measurement is the establishment of a classical record — an irreversible information transfer that resolves quantum superpositions into definite outcomes. The observer does not cause collapse; the observer IS the system for which collapse is registered. This IS the Tantraloka's pratyabhijñā (recognition): measurement IS consciousness knowing itself through a specific configuration. The 'collapse' IS the moment of recognition — not physical but cognitive.

gaps: Full extraction of all 6 papers needed
